summary.verdict_desc: 本周计数 7 红 / 6 黄 / 11 绿，红灯比例由上周 20.8% 升至 29.2%，加权风险分由 37.5% 升至 41.7%——这是近两月最明显的一次风险上移。驱动来自宏观侧两盏黄灯同时转红：(1) 资本开支指引市场反应（capex_reaction）由'偏奖励'转为'系统性惩罚'：6/22–27 当周 AI 板块遭遇 2026 年最大回调，纳指单日跌逾 4%、半导体板块市值蒸发约 1.3 万亿美元，华尔街明确从'奖励 AI 投入'转向'要求拿出回报证据'，NVDA 周二跌 3%、年内累计 -18%，B200 租赁价三周内由 $6.11 跌至 $4.22（-31%）施压算力定价——本轮非业绩暴雷而是估值与回报质疑驱动的系统性重定价，转红；(2) Fed 政策方向（fed_policy）由'维持高利率'转为'隐含加息'：6/17 点阵图中位数升至 3.8%（高于当前 3.50–3.75%）、9 名委员预期年内至少加息一次，通胀预期上修至 3.6%/核心 3.3%，市场对年内加息押注由一周前的 57% 跳升至约 90%、BofA 预计年内加息三次至 4.25–4.5%，方向已实质转鹰，转红（7 月会议仍以约 69% 概率维持，但政策方向明确向上）。估值依旧极度拉伸：CAPE 约 40.3（GuruFocus 39.64、MacroRadar 41.02）、前 5 大权重约 28.8%，唯 NVDA 远期 PE 随股价回落至约 22x（区间 19–24）反而更便宜、维持绿灯。信用面仍是最强支撑：HY 利差 6/25 约 278bps（较上周 271 略走阔但仍远低于均值、绿区），数据中心 ABS 利差近两周收窄约 60bps、发行火热，Neocloud 90 日零信用事件（但 CoreWeave 未来 12 个月 $9.7B 到期、总债务 $14B 为潜在隐患）。基本面继续兑现：OpenRouter token 量稳在约 100 万亿/月（6 月初周量已超 33 万亿）、Anthropic 5 月 ARR 约 $47B（4 月 $30B、约 6 周翻倍）领先 OpenAI $33B、二阶导仍正，Oracle RPO 维持 $600B+ 量级。基础判读为'中度警戒'（red_pct 29.2% 落在 25–40% 区间），但分类强制升级再次生效：估值 2/3 红（CAPE、Top-5）叠加资金面 3/4 红（Mag4 FCF 深度负值、VC AI 占比约 80%、NVDA 客户投资/收入约 43%）。估值 + 资金面双类共振触发分类强制升级，最终判读维持'高风险预警'。
summary.verdict_desc_en: This week prints 7 red / 6 yellow / 11 green, with the red share rising from 20.8% to 29.2% and the weighted risk score climbing from 37.5% to 41.7% — the sharpest risk-ward move in nearly two months. The driver is two macro yellow lights flipping red at once: (1) the capex-guidance market reaction (capex_reaction) shifted from 'rewarding delivery' to 'systematic punishment' as the AI complex suffered 2026's biggest correction during June 22-27 — the Nasdaq fell more than 4% in a single session and semiconductors shed roughly $1.3T in market value, with Wall Street explicitly pivoting from rewarding AI spend to demanding evidence of returns; NVDA fell 3% on Tuesday and is down 18% YTD, while B200 rental rates dropped from $6.11 to $4.22 (-31%) in three weeks, pressuring compute pricing — a valuation/return-driven repricing rather than an earnings miss, flips red; (2) Fed policy direction (fed_policy) moved from 'higher-for-longer hold' to 'implied hike' as the June 17 dot plot lifted the median to 3.8% (above the current 3.50-3.75%), with 9 members now penciling at least one 2026 hike and the inflation outlook raised to 3.6% headline / 3.3% core; market odds of a hike by year-end jumped from 57% a week ago to ~90%, and BofA now calls for three hikes to 4.25-4.5% — direction has turned genuinely hawkish, flips red (the July meeting is still ~69% hold, but the policy vector points up). Valuations remain extremely stretched: CAPE ~40.3 (GuruFocus 39.64, MacroRadar 41.02) and the Top-5 weight ~28.8%; only NVDA's forward PE, falling to ~22x (range 19-24) as the stock dropped, looks cheaper and stays green. Credit is still the strongest support: HY OAS was ~278bps on June 25 (a touch wider than last week's 271 but far below average, green), data-center ABS spreads tightened ~60bps over recent weeks amid heavy issuance, and neocloud credit logged zero events in 90 days (though CoreWeave's $9.7B of maturities within 12 months and $14B total debt remain latent risks). Fundamentals keep delivering: OpenRouter token volume holds near 100T/month (33T+ weekly by early June), Anthropic's ARR reached ~$47B in May (from $30B in April, doubling roughly every six weeks), ahead of OpenAI's ~$33B with a still-positive second derivative, and Oracle's RPO remains in the $600B+ range. The base framework reads Moderate Caution (red_pct 29.2%, in the 25-40% band), but the category override fires again: valuation 2/3 RED (CAPE, Top-5) plus capital 3/4 RED (Mag4 FCF deeply negative, VC AI share ~80%, NVDA customer-financing ~43%). Valuation + capital dual-category resonance triggers the forced upgrade, holding the verdict at High Risk Alert.

wow_changes.note: 6/22–27 当周 AI 板块遭 2026 年最大回调：纳指单日跌逾 4%、半导体板块市值蒸发约 1.3 万亿美元；华尔街由'奖励 AI 投入'转向'要求回报证据'，NVDA 周二 -3%、年内 -18%，B200 租赁价三周 -31%——市场系统性惩罚 AI 资本开支，由黄转红
wow_changes.note_en: The AI complex saw 2026's biggest correction during June 22-27: the Nasdaq fell 4%+ in one session and semiconductors lost ~$1.3T in market value; Wall Street pivoted from rewarding AI spend to demanding proof of returns, NVDA fell 3% Tuesday (down 18% YTD) and B200 rental rates dropped 31% in three weeks — the market is now systematically punishing AI capex, flips red

wow_changes.note: Fed 政策方向实质转鹰：6/17 点阵图中位数升至 3.8%（高于当前 3.50–3.75%）、9 名委员预期年内至少加息一次，通胀预期上修至 3.6%/核心 3.3%；市场对年内加息押注由一周前 57% 跳升至约 90%、BofA 预计加息三次至 4.25–4.5%，由'维持高利率'转为'隐含加息'，由黄转红
wow_changes.note_en: Fed policy direction turned genuinely hawkish: the June 17 dot plot lifted the median to 3.8% (above the current 3.50-3.75%), 9 members now see at least one 2026 hike, and the inflation outlook was raised to 3.6% headline / 3.3% core; market odds of a year-end hike jumped from 57% a week ago to ~90% and BofA calls for three hikes to 4.25-4.5% — shifts from higher-for-longer hold to implied hike, flips red

indicators.note: CAPE 约 40.3（GuruFocus 39.64、MacroRadar 41.02），随本周回调小幅回落但仍是 155 年来第 2 高、距 2000 年峰值 44.2 仅约 9%；远高于长期均值约 32，整体估值仍处极度拉伸区，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: CAPE sits near 40.3 (GuruFocus 39.64, MacroRadar 41.02), edging down with this week's pullback but still the 2nd-highest in 155 years and only ~9% below the 2000 peak of 44.2; far above its ~32 long-run average, valuations remain extremely stretched — stays red

indicators.note: S&P 500 前 5 大（NVDA 7.90% / AAPL 6.78% / GOOGL 6.03% / MSFT 4.37% / AMZN 3.71%）合计约 28.79%，与上周基本持平；尽管本周半导体重挫，集中度仍贴近 2000 年极值，市值高度押注少数 AI 龙头，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: The S&P 500's Top-5 (NVDA 7.90% / AAPL 6.78% / GOOGL 6.03% / MSFT 4.37% / AMZN 3.71%) total ~28.79%, roughly flat on the week; despite the semiconductor rout, concentration still hugs the 2000 extreme with the index heavily bet on a handful of AI leaders — stays red

indicators.note: NVDA 远期 PE 约 22x（GuruFocus 21.95、Yahoo 21.93、FinanceCharts 24.04、StockAnalysis 19.37，区间 19–24x），随本周股价回调（周二 -3%、年内 -18%）进一步降至历史低位；EPS 持续上修使倍数仍远低于 12 月均值约 43，NVDA 自身估值并非泡沫源头，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: NVDA's forward PE is ~22x (GuruFocus 21.95, Yahoo 21.93, FinanceCharts 24.04, StockAnalysis 19.37; range 19-24x), pressed further to historic lows by this week's drop (-3% Tuesday, -18% YTD); ongoing EPS upgrades keep the multiple far below its ~43 12-month average — NVDA's own valuation is not the bubble's source, stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 指引下调=红 / 加速=黄 / 稳健=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Guidance cut = red / Accelerating = yellow / Steady = green
indicators.note: 四大 hyperscaler 2026 capex 合计约 $725B（AMZN $200B、MSFT $190B、GOOGL $175-185B、META 上调至 $125-145B），YoY +77%，无一下调指引；MSFT 披露约 $80B Azure 订单因电力受限无法交付、需求仍超供给；继续加速、不可持续特征明显，但本周市场已开始质疑回报，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: The four hyperscalers guide ~$725B of 2026 capex (AMZN $200B, MSFT $190B, GOOGL $175-185B, META raised to $125-145B), +77% YoY, with none cutting guidance; MSFT flagged ~$80B of Azure orders it cannot fill on power constraints as demand outstrips supply — still accelerating with clear unsustainability hallmarks, though the market began questioning returns this week, stays yellow

indicators.note: 自由现金流已停止增长、资本开支持续攀升，Mag7 叙事由'造现金'转向'吞现金'；Amazon 季度 FCF 已转负（-$12.4B、-$8.4B），全年料深度为负，MSFT FCF 由 $20.3B 降至 $15.8B；capex 持续吞噬现金流、需外部融资支撑，无季度新数据沿用上季口径，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: Free cash flow has stopped growing while capex keeps climbing, shifting the Mag7 story from cash generation to cash consumption; Amazon's quarterly FCF has turned negative (-$12.4B, -$8.4B) and is set deeply negative for the year, while MSFT's FCF fell from $20.3B to $15.8B; capex keeps devouring cash and leans on external financing — no new quarterly print, carrying the prior read, stays red

indicators.name_zh: AI 占 VC 投资比重
indicators.note: Crunchbase/PitchBook Q1 2026：全球 VC 创纪录约 $297-300B，AI 占 80-81%（$242-255B），较 2025 Q1 的 55% 大幅跃升；OpenAI/Anthropic/xAI 三笔大单占 67%、美元高度集中于少数巨额轮次；Q2 尚无完整新数据沿用 Q1 口径，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: Crunchbase/PitchBook Q1 2026: global VC hit a record ~$297-300B with AI taking 80-81% ($242-255B), up sharply from 55% in Q1 2025; OpenAI/Anthropic/xAI alone made up 67% as dollars concentrate in a few mega-rounds; no complete Q2 print yet, carrying the Q1 read — stays red

indicators.note: NVDA 2026 年至今 AI 股权承诺逾 $40B（含 OpenAI $30B 直投、由 $100B 意向缩减，Corning 至多 $3.2B、IREN $2.1B 等七笔上市公司大单 + 二十余笔私募轮）；累计承诺再加 GPU 抵押债务，对 LTM 收入约 $300B 的比率约 43%，远超 Lucent 1999 峰值 24%，每笔投资多捆绑采购协议、循环融资特征明显，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: NVDA has committed $40B+ of AI equity so far in 2026 (incl. a $30B direct OpenAI stake trimmed from a $100B LOI, plus seven public-company stakes such as up to $3.2B for Corning and $2.1B for IREN and two dozen private rounds); against ~$300B LTM revenue that is ~43%, far above Lucent's 1999 peak of 24%, with most deals bundled to purchase agreements — circular-financing hallmarks persist, stays red

indicators.note: S5FI 6/28 约 54.07（上周约 55.3，近期区间 54-56），仍落在 40-60% 黄区；本周半导体重挫但回调集中于头部 AI/芯片股、广度未崩，参与度偏弱但未触红，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: S5FI was ~54.07 on June 28 (≈55.3 last week, recent range 54-56), still inside the 40-60% yellow band; this week's semiconductor rout was concentrated in mega-cap AI/chip names so breadth did not collapse — participation is soft but not red, stays yellow

indicators.name_zh: 市值加权 vs 等权重
indicators.note: 等权重 RSP 继续领先市值加权 SPY：RSP YTD +9.96% vs SPY +8.48%，收益差约 -1.5% 仍落在 <5% 绿区；本周大盘科技回调反而强化等权优势、过去一月 RSP +2.49% 而 SPY 近乎持平，收益广度仍均衡，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Equal-weight RSP keeps leading cap-weighted SPY: RSP +9.96% YTD vs SPY +8.48%, a ~-1.5% spread still inside the sub-5% green zone; this week's mega-cap tech pullback actually reinforced the equal-weight edge (RSP +2.49% over the past month vs roughly flat SPY) — return breadth stays balanced, stays green

indicators.name_zh: AI 龙头内部人卖买比
indicators.note: AI 硬件链内部人买入'近乎消失'：6/17 NVDA 五名最高管理层同日抛售约 $4000 万（Huang 约 $950 万、Kress 约 $850 万），过去 3 个月 NVDA 内部人净卖约 $410.6M、累计已超 $3.3B（Huang 一人约 $2.9B）；个人层面仍以卖出为主、卖买比偏高但未及 2000 年 23x 极值，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: Insider buying across the AI-hardware complex has 'nearly vanished': on June 17 NVDA's five most senior officers sold ~$40M on the same day (Huang ~$9.5M, Kress ~$8.5M), with NVDA insiders net-selling ~$410.6M over the past three months and ~$3.3B cumulatively (Huang alone ~$2.9B); flows stay sell-heavy and the ratio is elevated but below the 2000 extreme of 23x — stays yellow

indicators.threshold_text: 洪流=红 / 升温=黄 / 平静=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Flood = red / Warming = yellow / Quiet = green
indicators.note: 管道继续升温但未成洪流：Cerebras（5/14 首挂涨 89%）与 SpaceX（6/12 挂牌）已完成，OpenAI 拟最早 2026 年底/2027 上市（估值约 $1T、年化收入约 $25B）、Anthropic 拟最早 10 月上市（拟估值约 $900B）、Databricks 料 H2 递表；已挂牌/临近发行的具体公司数仍 <10，且本周大盘回调或令窗口承压，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: The pipeline keeps warming but is short of a Flood: Cerebras (debuted +89% on May 14) and SpaceX (listed June 12) are done, while OpenAI eyes a late-2026/2027 listing (~$1T target, ~$25B annualized revenue), Anthropic a listing as early as October (~$900B target), and Databricks an H2 filing; the count of listed/imminent names is still under 10 and this week's pullback may pressure the window — stays yellow

indicators.note: ICE BofA HY OAS 6/25 约 2.78%（278 bps），较上周约 271bps 略走阔但仍远低于长期均值 3.94%、中位数 4.52%；尽管股市大幅波动且 AI capex 持续烧钱，信用市场对 AI 风险仍未现压力，是当前最支撑多头的指标，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: ICE BofA HY OAS was ~2.78% (278 bps) on June 25, a touch wider than last week's ~271bps but still far below the 3.94% long-run average and 4.52% median; despite sharp equity volatility and ongoing AI capex burn, credit markets show no stress on AI risk — the most bull-supportive indicator right now, stays green

indicators.name_zh: 数据中心 ABS 利差
indicators.threshold_text: 走阔 50bps+ = 红 / 稳定 = 黄 / 收窄 = 绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widening 50bps+ = red / Stable = yellow / Tightening = green
indicators.note: 数据中心 ABS 条件持续宽松：顶级评级新发利差近两周收窄约 60bps，单周发行一度逾 $14.5 亿、全年发行规模由 2020 年 $2.6B 升至逾 $14B；S&P/Fitch/KBRA 对 Meta Hyperion 等在建项目基于租户担保给予投资级评级，信用市场对 AI 基建仍未现压力，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Data-center ABS conditions stay accommodative: top-rated new-issue spreads tightened ~60bps over recent weeks, weekly issuance topped $1.45B at one point, and annual volume has grown from $2.6B in 2020 to over $14B; S&P/Fitch/KBRA have granted investment-grade ratings on under-construction projects such as Meta's Hyperion on tenant guarantees — no credit stress on AI infrastructure, stays green

indicators.note: CoreWeave / Nebius / Crusoe / Lambda 90 日内无评级下调或违约；Nebius Q3 营收 +355%、与 Meta 新签 5 年 $3B 协议，CoreWeave 季营收 $1.4B（+134%）；但 CoreWeave 总债务约 $14B、未来 12 个月 $9.7B 到期，杠杆与到期集中仍是潜在隐患——短期信用零事件，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: CoreWeave / Nebius / Crusoe / Lambda — no downgrades or defaults in 90 days; Nebius posted +355% revenue and a new 5-year $3B deal with Meta, while CoreWeave booked $1.4B quarterly revenue (+134%); but CoreWeave's ~$14B total debt and $9.7B of maturities within 12 months keep leverage and maturity concentration a latent risk — with zero near-term events it stays green

indicators.name_zh: 全口径债务/Capex 流量比
indicators.note: 自下而上拆解（流量口径）：分子=2026E 全口径 AI 新增债务，Morgan Stanley 估全年约 $570B（含 hyperscaler 及 JV $250-300B、neoclouds 与 Meta Hyperion/Oracle Stargate 等表外 SPV）；分母=2026E 全口径 AI 基建 capex 约 $1.0-1.1T（五大 hyperscaler 含 Oracle ~$805B + neoclouds/其他）。$570B ÷ ~$1.05T ≈ 54%（区间 48-60%），仍低于互联网/电信基建泡沫顶 80-100%，列中度警戒（2026-06-28 周期内无新数据，沿用 2026-06-14 拆解）。
indicators.note_en: Bottom-up, flow basis: numerator = 2026E all-in new AI debt, Morgan Stanley estimates ~$570B for the year (incl. $250-300B of hyperscaler + JV issuance and off-balance-sheet SPVs such as Meta Hyperion and Oracle Stargate); denominator = 2026E all-in AI-infrastructure capex ~$1.0-1.1T (the five hyperscalers incl. Oracle ~$805B plus neoclouds/others). ~$570B ÷ ~$1.05T ≈ 54% (range 48-60%), still below the 80-100% peak of the dot-com / telecom build-outs — scored moderate (no new data within the cycle as of 2026-06-28, carrying the 2026-06-14 breakdown).
indicators.source_name: Morgan Stanley + 自下而上拆解

indicators.name_zh: AI 行业 Token 月度环比
indicators.threshold_text: 收缩=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Contraction = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: OpenRouter 月 token 量稳在约 100 万亿（周约 25 万亿、半年 5 倍），6 月初第三方追踪周量已超 33 万亿、单日逾 1 万亿；agentic 推理为最快增长来源、服务超 800 万用户，全产业用量继续加速，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: OpenRouter holds at ~100T tokens/month (~25T weekly, 5x in six months), with third-party trackers placing weekly volume above 33T and daily volume over 1T by early June; agentic inference is the fastest-growing driver and the platform serves 8M+ users — industry-wide usage keeps accelerating, stays green

indicators.name_zh: Token 增速 / 收入增速 比值
indicators.note: Anthropic ARR 由 4 月约 $30B 升至 5 月约 $47B（环比约 +50%），收入增速持续快于 token 量增速（半年 5 倍、月环比约 +35%）；单 token 价值上行、付费需求真实，比值维持 <1x，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Anthropic's ARR rose from ~$30B in April to ~$47B in May (~+50% MoM), with revenue growth still outpacing token-volume growth (5x in six months, ~+35% MoM); per-token value keeps rising on genuine paid demand, holding the ratio below 1x — stays green

indicators.name_zh: AI 收入增速的二阶导
indicators.threshold_text: 减速=红 / 平稳=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Decel = red / Flat = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: Anthropic run-rate 由 4 月约 $30B 升至 5 月约 $47B，月增量持续放大、约 6 周翻倍（2025 年底 $9B → 当前 $47B），已领先 OpenAI 约 $33B，二阶导维持正向；唯 Anthropic 含云分销毛收入口径仍存争议，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Anthropic's run-rate climbed from ~$30B in April to ~$47B in May, with the monthly increment still expanding (doubling roughly every six weeks, $9B at end-2025 to ~$47B now) and now ahead of OpenAI's ~$33B — the 2nd derivative stays positive, though Anthropic's gross cloud-reseller revenue basis remains contested; stays green

indicators.note: McKinsey Q1 2026：72% 企业至少 1 项 AI workload 生产化（2024 55%、2020 20%）；但仅约 28% 已规模化生产、不足 40% 突破试点，Gartner 预计年底 40% 企业应用嵌入 AI agent；头部部署率高位但深度集成仍在爬坡，无季度新调查沿用，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: McKinsey Q1 2026: 72% of enterprises run at least one AI workload in production (vs 55% in 2024, 20% in 2020); but only ~28% have scaled in production and under 40% have moved beyond pilots, while Gartner expects 40% of enterprise apps to embed AI agents by year-end — the headline rate stays high though deep integration is still climbing; no new quarterly survey, carried over, stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 负增长=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Negative = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: Oracle FY26 全年 RPO 由 Q1 $455B 逐季抬升至 Q4 $638B（+363% YoY），与 OpenAI/xAI/Meta/Nvidia/AMD 多笔数十亿级合约绑定；MSFT 商业 RPO 约为 Oracle 2.4 倍、GCP 积压持续扩张，合计积压超 $1.4T，递延需求强劲；但 Oracle 逾 50% RPO 来自 OpenAI，强需求与客户集中并存，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Oracle's FY26 RPO rose each quarter from $455B in Q1 to $638B in Q4 (+363% YoY), tied to multi-billion-dollar contracts with OpenAI/xAI/Meta/Nvidia/AMD; MSFT's commercial RPO is ~2.4x Oracle's and GCP's backlog keeps expanding, bringing the combined pipeline above $1.4T as deferred demand stays robust; but over 50% of Oracle's RPO is from OpenAI — strong demand alongside concentration, stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 任何=红 / 调查=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Any confirmed = red / Investigation = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: 本周无主要 AI 龙头新增正式执法；Super Micro 早前的会计/出口管制问题非核心 AI 龙头，Burry 持续质疑 Mag7 借延长 GPU 折旧（2-3 年实际、5-6 年账面）虚增利润但属观点而非执法；SEC 对 AI-washing 关注上升、年初对某数据情报公司 round-tripping 执法，但均非当前核心 AI 龙头，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: No new formal enforcement against a major AI bellwether this week; Super Micro's earlier accounting/export issues are not a core AI leader, and Burry keeps alleging Mag7 inflates profits via extended GPU depreciation (2-3 year real life booked over 5-6 years) — a view, not an enforcement action; the SEC's focus on AI-washing is rising (with an early-2026 round-tripping case against a data-intelligence firm), but none target current core AI leaders — stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 加息=红 / 通胀压力=黄 / 降息=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Hike = red / Inflation pressure = yellow / Cut = green
indicators.note: Fed 政策方向本周实质转鹰：6/17 点阵图中位数升至 3.8%（高于当前 3.50-3.75%）、9 名委员预期年内至少加息一次，通胀预期上修至 3.6%/核心 3.3%（3 月为 2.7%）；市场对年内加息押注由一周前约 57% 跳升至约 90%、BofA 预计加息三次至 4.25-4.5%，10 年期收益率走高。虽 7 月会议仍约 69% 概率维持，但政策向量已明确转向加息，对极度拉伸的估值构成实质压制，由黄转红
indicators.note_en: Fed policy direction turned genuinely hawkish this week: the June 17 dot plot lifted the median to 3.8% (above the current 3.50-3.75%), 9 members now see at least one 2026 hike, and the inflation outlook was raised to 3.6% headline / 3.3% core (from 2.7% in March); market odds of a year-end hike jumped from ~57% a week ago to ~90%, BofA projects three hikes to 4.25-4.5%, and 10-year yields climbed. Although the July meeting is still ~69% hold, the policy vector has clearly shifted toward hiking — a real headwind for extremely stretched valuations, flips red

indicators.name_zh: 资本开支指引市场反应
indicators.threshold_text: 系统性惩罚=红 / 偶发=黄 / 奖励=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Systematic punishment = red / Episodic = yellow / Rewarded = green
indicators.note: 市场反应本周由'偏奖励'急转'系统性惩罚'：6/22-27 当周 AI 板块遭 2026 年最大回调，纳指单日跌逾 4%、半导体板块市值蒸发约 1.3 万亿美元，华尔街明确由'奖励 AI 投入'转向'要求拿出回报证据'；触发因素含 capex 强度达营收 34%（逾 1990 年代互联网建设峰值两倍）、B200 租赁价三周 -31%、加息预期升温。非业绩暴雷而是估值与回报质疑驱动的系统性重定价，由黄转红
indicators.note_en: The market reaction lurched from 'rewarding delivery' to 'systematic punishment' this week: during June 22-27 the AI complex suffered 2026's biggest correction, with the Nasdaq down 4%+ in a single session and semiconductors shedding ~$1.3T in market value, as Wall Street pivoted from rewarding AI spend to demanding evidence of returns; triggers included capex intensity reaching 34% of revenue (more than double the 1990s internet-build peak), B200 rental rates falling 31% in three weeks, and rising rate-hike expectations. Not an earnings miss but a valuation/return-driven systematic repricing — flips red

indicators.threshold_text: 普遍承认过热=红 / 部分=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widespread acknowledgment = red / Partial = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: 对冲表态持续：Altman 直言小盘 AI 估值'非理性、insane'、'必有人巨亏'；Dimon 称'far more worried than others'、'不少钱很可能会亏'、视美股被 AI 推至过热；Burry 称延长 GPU 折旧为'当代最常见骗局之一'；但 Dimon、Pichai 等仍强调 AI 长期'会兑现'、CEO 未集体收手，未达全体一致承认过热，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: Hedging persists: Altman calls small-cap AI valuations 'irrational, insane' and warns 'someone will lose a phenomenal amount'; Dimon says he is 'far more worried than others,' that 'a lot of the money will probably be lost,' and sees US markets overheated by AI; Burry calls extended GPU depreciation 'one of the more common frauds of the modern era'; yet Dimon and Pichai still insist AI 'will pay off' long term and CEOs have not collectively pulled back — short of a universal 'overheated' consensus, stays yellow
